title: crataegus monogyna subsp. inermis
intro: Crataegus monogyna subsp. inermis, also known as the thornless hawthorn, is a subspecies of the hawthorn tree (Crataegus monogyna), characterized by its lack of thorns and dense, white flowers. The medicinal properties of Crataegus monogyna subsp. inermis lie in its rich content of flavonoids, saponins, and oligomeric proanthocyanidin complexes, which have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardiovascular benefits. The health benefits of this plant include lowering blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, reducing the risk of heart disease, and supporting digestive health. Crataegus monogyna subsp. inermis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote overall well-being and heart health.
